coefficient.def: 係數、共同作用的人的人的人物 (單數)
coefficient.ex: The correlation coefficient between variables was low.

coequal.def: 同等的、同等的人的人的人物 (同)
coequal.ex: They worked as coequal partners in the law firm.

coerce.def: 強制、威脅、脅迫的人的人
coerce.ex: They tried to coerce the witness into lying tonight.

coercion.ex: The government used economic coercion to win power.

coercive.def: 強制的、威脅的人的人物
coercive.ex: The police had to use coercive measures for safety.

coexist.def: 同時存在、共處的人的人
coexist.ex: Different local groups must learn to coexist tonight.

coexistence.ex: Both nations hope for a long peaceful coexistence.

coextensive.ex: The two ancient empires had coextensive periods.

coexecutor.ex: He was named co-executor of his uncle's large will.

coffee.col: coffee shop, white coffee, coffee filter, instant coffee
coffee.ex: I'd like a large white coffee with no sugar tonight.

coffeepot.ex: She poured several cups from the silver coffeepot.

coffer.ex: The taxes helped to fill the local public coffers.

coffin.def: 棺材、裝入棺中的人的人
coffin.col: wooden coffin, nail in the coffin (致命打擊)
coffin.ex: The heavy wooden coffin was carried by six men.

cog.def: 齒輪、(俚)小成員的人物 (單數)
cog.ex: He felt like just a small cog in the big machine.

cogency.def: 說服力、中肯的人物 (單數)
cogency.ex: The report was praised for its analytical cogency.

cogent.def: 使人信服的、中肯的人的人物
cogent.ex: She made a very cogent argument for local change.

cogently.ex: The scientist argued cogently for more research.

cogitate.def: 深思、認真思考的人的人
cogitate.ex: She sat quietly to cogitate on her future plans.

cogitation.ex: After deep cogitation, he finally made a decision.

cognac.def: 康涅克白蘭地酒的人物 (單數)
cognac.ex: He enjoyed a small glass of cognac after dinner.

cognate.def: 同源的、血親的人的人物 (單數)
cognate.ex: English and German are both local cognate languages.

cognition.ex: The study examined the complex process of cognition.

cognitive.def: 認知的、認識的人的人物
cognitive.ex: Toys are essential for early cognitive development.

cognizance/cognisance.def: 認知、認定、審理的人物 (單數)
cognizance/cognisance.ex: The judge will take cognizance of the new facts.

cognizant/cognisant.def: 認知的、曉得的人的人物
cognizant/cognisant.ex: He is fully cognizant of the risks involved today.

cognomen.def: 姓、綽號、名字的人物 (單數)
cognomen.ex: Cicero was a famous roman cognomen from history.

cohabit.ex: They decided to cohabit together in a small flat.

cohabitation.ex: Long-term cohabitation is common in this city.

cohere.def: 凝聚、連貫、接合的人的人
cohere.ex: The different parts of story didn't cohere well.

coherence.def: 凝聚、連貫、一致的人物 (單數)
coherence.ex: His strange speech suffered from a lack of coherence.

coherent.def: 連貫的、凝聚的人的人物
coherent.ex: She made a very coherent and logical argument.

coherently.ex: He explained all complex theories very coherently.

cohesion.def: 凝聚、結合、團結的人物 (單數)
cohesion.ex: The local club promotes social cohesion in town.

cohesive.def: 凝聚性的、團結的人的人物
cohesive.ex: They are a very cohesive and hard-working group.
